REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS, PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SHALL REMAIN IN EFFECT ON THE TERMS LISTED IN OCTOBER 25, 2016 ORDER UNTIL FURTHER ORDER OF THE COURT. PARTIES ARE TO PROMPTLY ARRANGE FOR A DBI INSPECTION. WITHIN 10 DAYS OF RECEIPT OF DBI REPORT, PLAINTIFF SHALL FILE AND CALENDAR PER CCP 1005 A HEARING ADDRESSING THE NEED FOR FURTHER INJUNCTION AND OTHER RELIEF, IF NECESSARY. ALL RENTS SHALL BE DEPOSITED INTO JOINT ACCOUNT WITHIN 2 BUSINESS DAYS OF RECEIPT UNTIL THE CASE IS RESOLVED. NO PAYMENTS/WITHDRAWALS OTHER THAN ORDINARY PROPERTY EXPENSES (E.G. TAXES, UTILITIES) SHALL BE MADE OUT OF THE JOINT ACCOUNT WITHOUT WRITTEN CONSENT OF BOTH PARTIES. HEARING REQUIRED TO ADDRESS DECEMBER 2016 RENT. DEFENDANT TO PROVIDE DECLARATION BY NO LATER THAN 9:00 A.M. ON DECEMBER 14, 2016 ADDRESSING (1) WHEN DECEMBER RENT WAS RECEIVED; (2) IN WHAT MANNER (CASH, CHECK, DIRECT DEPOSIT, ETC.); (3) WHEN AND WHERE IS HAS BEEN DEPOSITED. PARTIES HAVE STIPULATED TO MODIFICATIONS AND WILL INCLUDE THE MODIFICATIONS IN THE PROPOSED ORDER. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

Off Calendar Nov-13-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Courtcall:
David Bloomenthal, Esq., 415-409-7611, appearing for Cara Vainish, Plaintiff
Matthew Klein, Defendant in Pro Per , 415-730-1922, appearing in person.

ARGUED. Mr. Bloomenthal stated that the agreement is severable, provided case background, addressed the issue of the approximate amount that Mr. Klein is to receive and the tenant who is still living at the premises. Mr. Klein stated that Mr. Bloomenthal was not involved in the negotiation process.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Dec-14-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Niles Rosenquest, 415-292-0980 for Defendant Matthew Klein.
Dean Rossi, 408-512-3376 for Plaintiff Cara Vanish.

9:34am Counsel requested time to meet and confer.

10:02am Counsel for both parties inform the Court and report to the Court that they have reached an agreement. The Court rules as follows: Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction is GRANTED as follows, Preliminary Injunction shall remain in effect on the terms listed in October 25, 2016 order until further order of the Court. Parties are to promptly arrange for a DBI inspection. Within 10 days of receipt of DBI report, Plaintiff shall file and calendar per CCP 1005 a hearing addressing the need for further injunction and other relief, if necessary. All rents shall be deposited into joint account within 2 business days of receipt until the case is resolved. No payments/withdrawals other than ordinary property expenses (e.g. taxes, utilities) shall be made out of the joint account without written consent of both parties. Hearing required to address December 2016 rent. Defendant to provide declaration by no later than 9:00 a.m. on December 14, 2016 addressing (1) when December rent was received; (2) in what manner (cash, check, direct deposit, etc.); (3) when and where is has been deposited. Parties have stipulated to modifications and will include the modifications in the proposed order. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
